_about: Single source of truth for every label + description rendered into the protocol log. Templates use {placeholder} for dynamic values substituted at runtime. The agent provider is spelled 'Agent Provider' (not 'AP') to avoid conflation with 'Access Server'. The person server is spelled 'Person Server' everywhere for consistency.

generate_ephemeral.label: Agent: generate signing key
generate_ephemeral.description: The agent creates a fresh signing keypair. The private half never leaves this device, so tokens issued later are useless to anyone else.

agent_provider_request.description: The agent posts to the Agent Provider's bootstrap endpoint with a sig=hwk request — its public key is in the Signature-Key header, and the body names the Person Server the user picked. The Agent Provider records the (key thumbprint → agent name) mapping in its KV and returns an agent_token bound to the key.

cannot_refresh.label: Cannot refresh
cannot_refresh.description: No saved key on this device, so there's nothing to refresh — a fresh bootstrap is needed.

agent_provider_request.description: The agent signs a refresh request with the same hwk key the Agent Provider already has on file. The Agent Provider looks up the agent name by thumbprint and mints a fresh agent_token bound to the same key.

missing_context.label: Missing agent_token or signing key
missing_context.description: The agent doesn't have an agent_token or key yet — bootstrap has to finish first.

agent_provider_authorize_request.description: The agent asks its Agent Provider for a resource token scoped to this Person Server and resource. The Agent Provider signs it on the agent's behalf.

ps_token_request.description: The agent trades that resource token with your Person Server for an auth token. A 200 means you've already consented to this scope; 202 means the Person Server needs your approval for a new one.

ps_pending_longpoll.description: If new consent is needed, the agent keeps one request open while you decide, instead of polling. The Person Server responds the moment you approve or deny.

ps_consent_prompt.label: User at Person Server: consent prompt
ps_consent_prompt.description: Your Person Server asks if this agent may use the requested scope. Approve here, or scan the QR to approve on another device.

ps_consent_prompt.label: User at Person Server: consent prompt (resumed)
ps_consent_prompt.description: You returned mid-approval. The agent picks up the same pending request instead of starting over.

resource_metadata_request.description: The agent fetches the resource's well-known metadata to discover the authorization endpoint plus the OpenAPI document describing the operations it can request.

openapi_request.description: The agent pulls the OpenAPI spec so it can render a checkbox per operationId — the protocol lets the agent ask for exactly the operations it needs.

authorize_request.description: The agent POSTs the operations it wants to the resource's authorize endpoint, signed with its agent_token. The resource responds with a resource_token naming an R3 document the Person Server will fetch during token exchange.

r3_document_request.description: The resource_token's r3_uri claim points at the R3 document the Person Server is about to fetch. In production this endpoint is gated on a PS HTTP signature; the notes resource leaves it publicly fetchable so the playground can preview the exact JSON the PS will receive.

ps_token_request.description: The agent trades the resource_token at the Person Server's token endpoint. A 200 means consent was already on file; a 202 triggers a consent prompt. The Person Server fetches the R3 document, then emits an auth_token carrying r3_granted — the operations it's releasing.

ps_pending_longpoll.description: If new consent is needed, the agent keeps a request open while you decide. The Person Server replies the moment you approve or deny.

ps_consent_prompt.label: User at Person Server: consent prompt
ps_consent_prompt.description: Your Person Server asks which of the requested operations to grant. Approve here, or scan the QR to approve on another device.

auth_token_received.label: Auth Token received
auth_token_received.description: The Person Server released an auth_token with r3_granted — the operations you actually approved. The agent stores this and uses it to sign every call to the Notes API.

ps_consent_prompt.label: User at Person Server: consent prompt (resumed)
ps_consent_prompt.description: You returned mid-approval of a Notes request. The agent picks up the same pending exchange instead of starting over.

list_request.description: The agent lists the user's notes, signing the request with the auth_token.

create_request.description: The agent creates a new note.

get_request.description: The agent reads a single note by id.

update_request.description: The agent updates an existing note. Saving resets the note's 24-hour expiry.

missing_key.label: Demo API Call Failed
missing_key.description: No signing key on this device — the demo call can't be signed, so it won't go out.

request.description: The agent calls the resource's demo endpoint, signing the HTTP request with its private key and attaching the auth token. The server checks the signature matches the token, then checks the token's scope covers this endpoint.

unhandled.label: Unhandled error
unhandled.description: Something went wrong that the flow wasn't expecting — check the console for details.

approve_at_ps.continue_label: Continue at your Person Server to approve this request
